error.signature: AccessDenied: User is not authorized to perform this action even after policy is attached

verdict.summary: IAM policy changes take up to 60 seconds to propagate globally due to eventual consistency. AWS docs mention this in a footnote but most tutorials show attach-then-use immediately.

dead_ends.action: Attach IAM policy and immediately make API call
dead_ends.why_fails: IAM is eventually consistent. Policy may take 10-60 seconds to propagate. First call almost always fails.

dead_ends.action: Detach and reattach the policy thinking it didn't apply
dead_ends.why_fails: Reattaching resets the propagation timer. Makes the problem worse.

dead_ends.action: Add a hardcoded sleep(5) after policy attachment
dead_ends.why_fails: 5 seconds is often not enough. Propagation can take up to 60 seconds in some regions.

workarounds.action: Implement retry with exponential backoff after IAM changes
workarounds.how: for i in range(6): try: client.action() except AccessDenied: time.sleep(2**i)

workarounds.action: Use STS AssumeRole to force fresh credential evaluation
workarounds.how: After attaching policy, assume the role again to get fresh session credentials

workarounds.action: Use IAM policy simulator API to verify propagation before proceeding
